observations.recommendation:
1.5 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to adhere to the proper
and correct classification of accounts through preparation of journal entries the
financial statements in accordance with the Chart of Accounts prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2015-009, to be able to maintain accurate and reliable accounting
records, and to ensure that the financial statements of the municipality are
presented fairly. Consequently, require the adjustment of the above enumerated
accounts and its corresponding accumulated depreciation to its appropriate
account titles.

2.8 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the necessary
adjustments in the books of the municipality dropping the costs of the barangay
structures and transfer the same to the books of the recipient barangays. Turn-
over documents of newly completed structure/s shall be attached to the JEV
transferring the cost of the structure/s.

3.12 We recommended the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments
in the books based on the corrected depreciation expense calculation. Henceforth,
prepare a lapsing schedule to accurately compute the depreciation of each
depreciable asset to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.
3.13 The Municipal Accountant agreed to the observation and committed make the necessary
adjustment in the books after the preparation of the lapsing schedule as recommended.

4.6 Require the Municipal Accountant to observe proper and correct use of accounts.
Use of accounts shall always be in accordance with the prescribed Chart of
Accounts to be able to maintain accurate and reliable accounting records, and to
ensure that the financial statements of the municipality are fairly presented.
4.7 The Municipal Accountant agreed to the observation and committed to implement the
audit recommendation. However, the Municipal Budget Officer specifically mentioned
that the payment of salaries of the Medical Technologist does not fall under the account
Other Professional Services based on the provided description of the accounts.
4.8 Other Professional Services is described as an account used to record the cost of other
professional services contracted by the agency not otherwise classified under any of
the specific professional services accounts. This includes honoraria paid by LGUs to
national government personnel (judges, prosecutors, public elementary and high school
teachers and other national government personnel) assigned in LGUs. This also
includes other operating expenses granted to national government agencies stationed in
the LGU.
4.9 It is the Audit Team’s contention that based on the cited description of the Other
Professional Services account, it is the most appropriate account to use in recording the
payments of salaries of the Medical Technologist as a Contract of Service employee.
The description did not limit the charges of the account to payments of allowances to
the national government personnel assigned in the LGU but it includes such payments.

5.4 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal
Accountant with an updated and reconciled list of delinquent real property
taxes to facilitate a detailed reconciliation of the unreconciled amount of
₱1,716,876.86, identify specific discrepancies, and make the necessary
adjustments.

6.5 We recommended that management require the Inventory Committee to
prioritize and ensure full implementation of the “One-time cleansing of PPE
accounts” in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.

7.7 We recommended the Municipal Accountant to ensure that cash advances for
payrolls shall be supported with payrolls or list of payees with their net amounts
pursuant to the afore-cited provision of COA Circular No. 97-002 to avoid the
grant of excessive cash advances.
7.8 We further recommended management to demand refund of the excess cash
advances upon completion of the pay period to prevent the funds from risk of
loss or misappropriation.

8.7 It is recommended that the LGU launch a comprehensive information campaign to
actively inform taxpayers about the ongoing tax amnesty program, its benefits, and
the available payment options. This campaign shall utilize various platforms,
including social media, local media outlets, public announcements, and community
outreach, to ensure maximum reach. Additionally, the LGU need to provide clear
and easily accessible guidance on tax obligations through workshops, seminars, and
online resources, while also strengthening internal controls to ensure the smooth
implementation of the program and efficient collection of taxes.

9.10 We recommended management to require the officials and employees to refund
the excess amount of government share remitted to the HDMF.
9.11 Management agreed to refund the amount of over-remittance and committed to collect
them from the employees concerned through payroll deduction.

10.8 We recommended that management implement a biometric attendance system in
addition to the logbook to effectively monitor employees’ attendance and ensure
they are rendering the eight hours service daily.
10.9 Management acknowledged that their current practice in the monitoring of attendance
is prone to discrepancies and does not provide the level of accuracy and reliability
afforded by a biometric system. In view of this, management is planning to use a
biometric system of attendance in 2026 to have an accurate and tamper-proof
attendance monitoring system. Management further reasoned that the
implementation of the biometric system of attendance was delayed due to budgetary
constraints.
10.10 Management is fully committed to complying with COA and CSC regulations. The
implementation of a biometric attendance system is being prioritized to enhance
transparency, accountability and employee productivity. Pending installation of the
biometric system of attendance, management is using the two logbooks to record the
attendance of Regular and Casual Plantilla employees and Job Order workers. The
logbook provides the names and signatures as well as the time of their arrivals to and
departures from office. The LCE issued Memorandum No. 6 dated March 31, 2025
prescribing the guidelines for the proper monitoring of attendance in the LGU using
the logbook. Copies of the memorandum and sample pages of the two logbooks were
furnished to this Office.

11.6 We recommended the Municipal officers and employees to stop double claim of
DTE to save the limited funds of the government. We also recommended the
Municipal Accountant to, henceforth, verify the claims of traveling expenses of
LGU officials and employees and disallow excess claims of DTE that are
inconsistent with the provisions of EO No. 77.

12.6 We recommended that the LSB submit documents proving the actual receipt of
copies of the SEF FY 2024 Budget by the Mayor’s Office. We also recommend
that henceforth, the LSB observe proper transmittal of copies of the SEF Budget
to the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and
Municipal Budget Officer by ensuring that receipt thereof are supported with
duly received transmittal letters with indicated names, signatures and dates of
receipt. Lastly, we recommend that the furnishing of copies of the SEF Annual
Budget be done not later than seven days after the approval of the SEF Budget, to
promote transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of the
SEF.

13.5 We recommended that the LSB submit the quarterly SEF utilization reports to
the DepEd Central Office, through the DepEd-Division of Bohol, copy furnished
the local sanggunian and DBM Regional Offices, as mandated. We further
recommend that an officer be designated for monitoring compliance with
submission deadlines to ensure proper accountability and transparency in the
utilization of the SEF.

14.5 We recommend that the Management require the concerned personnel to furnish
the Auditor copies of Perfected Contracts and Purchase Orders within five
working days from execution thereof on keeping with the policy on the
transparency of all government transactions in compliance with COA Circular No.
2009-001, dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant adhere to the provisions of Section 44 of IPSAS 12 and Section 121, Volume I of NGAs Manual for LGUs in the accounting of inventories.  Detailed inventory records (Supplies Ledger Card) for each inventory item shall be prepared and maintained to established accurate account balances of inventories.

We recommended the management to retrieved the duly received transmittal letters of the liquidation of cash advances for confidential funds from the HoA/authorized liaison officer to effect the liquidation in the books.  We also recommend the submission of the remaining travel expenses vouchers related to the submission of liquidation reports to the ICFAU.

We recommended to management the immediate implementation of the financial assistance received, ensure that funds are utilized as intended, and abide by the terms and conditions stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement executed between the source and implementing agencies to maximize the benefit/s that could be derive from the projects.
We also recommended the Municipal Treasurer to return the duly verified unused and excess funds especially those long outstanding accounts to the source agencies pursuant to Section 67 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to recognize revenue for all donated/transferred assets from other government agencies and private institutions which results in increase in net assets in adherence to IPSAS 23 to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to effect the following adjusting journal entries to reconcile the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts:

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to effect the following adjusting journal entries to recognize the revenues earned and to correct the erroneous use of accounts to present fairly the financial statements as of December 31, 2023:
Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper recording of accounts and transactions in accordance with existing accounting rules and regulations to avoid inaccurate and misleading information in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the transactions for the operations of the cemetery and waterwork system in subsidiary ledgers maintained for these special accounts and to prepare the corresponding financial statements thereof to form part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balances as required under Section 105 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment in the books using the following adjusting journal entry:
We also recommend the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of correcting entries by immediately changing the accounts.  In all instances, adjustments shall be made by drawing a separate JEV to correct the erroneous entries.
Lastly, we recommend management to require written explanation from the accounting system provider on the glitch noted of the system which allowed direct changing of accounts to correct erroneous entries which implicate the credibility and reliability of the reports generated from the system.

We recommended that management instruct the Human Resource Office to update the functions of the IAS pursuant to the mandates of the DBM and communicate the same to the concerned offices for awareness and compliance.
Furthermore, the duties and functions of newly created position/s shall be carefully evaluated and see to it that it is accordance with the mandates of the plantilla position created for effectiveness in the performance of duties and responsibilities attach to the position.

We recommended that management stop the delegation of unrelated duties and responsibilities to the Internal Auditor and prioritize the discharge of the intended function of the Internal Audit Service.
We also recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO), per discussion with the concerned employee, formulate an Individual Development Plan (IDP) to document the needed steps that will be undertaken to improve or enhance performance of individual employees in order to address identified performance gaps and provide opportunities for enhancing competencies.
Moreover, we recommended that the Internal Auditor attend seminars on government expenditures and Republic Act No. 9184, and join capacity-building trainings on Internal Auditing Standards for the Philippine Public Sector (IASPPS) and on Internal Audit Manual (IAM) for LGUs in order to fully discharge its mandated functions.

Unserviceable government vehicle and vehicles which were turned-over to the barangays totaling Ᵽ2,300,000.00 are still booked up in the books of accounts resulting in the overstatement of the related accounts that affects fair presentation of the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: Non-submission of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) and the Monthly Report on the Utilization of LDRRMF as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 hindered the timely review of expenditures

observations.recommendation: Some appointed barangay officials and non-members of the Barangay Health and Emergency Rescue Team (BHERT) were paid the Special Risk Allowance (SRA) amounting to Ᵽ909.10 despite conflict of schedule for rendition of service at the barangay hall contrary to Section 2.2 of CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 -2004 resulting to irregular disbursement of funds.

observations.recommendation: Net take-home pay of some employees were below the mandated minimum amount of ₱5,000.00 contrary to provisions of Section 55 of the General Appropriations Act (GAA) for Fiscal Year 2022.

observations.recommendation: Appropriation for the 20% Development Fund (DF) was not optimally utilized contrary to Section 5.0 of DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2020-1; while continuing appropriations for this fund was not reviewed and reverted, and to be re-appropriated for other development projects, inconsistent with Paragraph 2, Section 322 of the Local Government Code, thereby depriving the constituents of vital services and benefits.

observations.recommendation: Additional cash advance was granted to the Disbursing Officer despite non-liquidation of the previous cash advance which is not in accordance with Item 4.1.2 of COA Circular 97-002, resulting in the accumulation of cash advance.

observations.recommendation: Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) acquired during the year totaling ₱1,793,117.79 were provided with depreciation computed for a full year contrary to IPSAS 17; thus, overstating depreciation expense and the Accumulated Depreciation by ₱730,069.06 and understating the asset and government equity accounts.

We recommended that Management demand the liquidation of the financial assistance granted to LGUS be made upon expected completion of the project or the intended purpose has been served in accordance with existing regulations and to refrain from extending grant to recipients with unliquidated financial assistance.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct on time the physical inventory of all its property, plant and equipment, and prepare and submit the RPCCPE not later than January 31 of the ensuing year, in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs Volume I. The Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee has to reconcile the balance of PPE per books with that of the inventory reports, verify and analyze the discrepancies, and prepare adjustments to the affected records.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the immediate cancellation/adjustment on the stale checks giving priority to long outstanding prior years’ issuance in order to reflect the correct balance of the asset account and the fair presentation of the financial statements at year end.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the interest earned on deposits of the Trust Fund to the General Fund as required under Section 122 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual Volume I.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to process claims on the advance payment/mobilization fee only upon submission to and acceptance by the procuring entity of an irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand as prescribed by Section 4.2 of Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the registration and the transfer of title of the land to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the Municipality of Dauis in compliance with Section 39 of PD 1445. Further, submit the lacking documents as required by COA Circular No. 2012-001 to substantiate the propriety and validity of the payment and to avoid suspension in audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate liquidation of cash advances through issuance of demand letters signed by him or authorized representative. Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code states in unequivocal terms that the failure of a public officer to have duly forthcoming any public funds or property with which he is chargeable upon demand by any duly authorized officer shall be prima facie evidence that he has put such missing funds or property to personal use.
We also recommend that the concerned officials adhere to COA Circular No. 97-002 especially the withholding of salaries of those who refuse to settle their accountabilities.

observations.reason: Withholding of salaries of accountable officers who fail to effect prompt liquidation was not strictly implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:
a.) Initiate the conduct of a complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties by creating an Inventory Committee composed of a representative each from the Mayor’s Office, from the General Services Office (or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office) and a representative from each department to prepare the necessary procedures in the proper and orderly conduct of the physical count of all the property, plant and equipment and inventories owned by the Municipality;
b.)  Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all the properties recorded in the books and those not yet recorded but actually found in the LGU offices/premises and to prepare the corresponding inventory reports thereof; and reconcile them with the accounting records to come up with the correct account balances in the financial statement.
c.) Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain stock, property and ledger cards, respectively to properly account the receipt and disposition of property and supplies. The Office of the Municipal Treasurer should request additional staff for the conduct of the physical inventory.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to conduct a complete physical inventory count
observations.reason: Management has already created an inventory committee however the committee failed to conduct inventory count for 2017 due to time constraint.

observations.recommendation:

b.)  Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify as Equipment the surveillance camera, movable tents, printer and submersible pump.  Require the issuance of Acknowledgment Receipt of Equipment (ARE) to pinpoint user’s accountability.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive issue an  order  to  the  PPE
inventory  committee  to  conduct  the  actual  physical  inventory  of  all
properties of the LGU and simultaneously perform the property tagging.


   We also recommend that the Accounting Office prepare the PPE ledger cards
in order to verify the balances reported in the inventory report.

We recommend Management to follow the guidelines on COA Circular 2015-
002 and COA Circular 2003-007 in computing depreciation expense of the PPE.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive advice the Accounting  Office
to undertake the retrieval of records or transaction documents of all  funds
transferred from other NGAs to the LGU that occurred prior  to  CY  2011  in
order to confirm the existence and validity of the payables.


   We further recommend that the Accounting Office prepare and maintain back-
up files of all its subsidiary ledgers and other  important  files  in  soft
and hard copies for easy referral in case of technical problems.

We recommend that henceforth the Municipal Accountant  shall  see  to  it
that the cash advance disbursement vouchers  and  supporting  documents  for
confidential  fund  be  submitted  within  the  prescribed  time  frame   as
promulgated in the DBM-DILG-DND-GCG-COA Joint Circular No. 2015-01.

We recommend that the concerned officials review and evaluate the  lined-
up activities for the Peace and Order Program in consonance with  item  3.23
of the Joint Circular No. 2015-01.  Further,  submit  legal  basis  for  the
inclusion of the abovementioned non-POP items.

We recommend that the management demand to the  concerned  employees  the
immediate liquidation of their cash  advances  and  implement  the  existing
rules and regulations  regarding  the  grant  and  liquidation  of  advances
including the withholding of salaries of accountable officers  who  fail  to
effect prompt liquidation.


   We also recommend that management make a follow-up to the Audit  Unit  on
the status of the liquidation reports submitted.

We recommend that Management submit the lacking documents and observe COA
Circular Nos. 2012-001 on the documentation requirements of fund transfers.

We recommend that management closely monitor the utilization of the Local
Disaster  Risk  Reduction  and  Management  Fund  and  ensure  that  planned
program/projects/activities are implemented.

We recommend that management require the BAC Chairman  to  post  all  the
Notices of  Awards  and  Notices  to  Proceed  together  with  the  approved
contracts for all procurements undertaken by the LGU in the PhilGEPS and  in
the agency website if any, also in any conspicuous place in premises of  the
municipality within  the  prescribed  period  in  compliance  with  Sections
37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the RIRR of R.A. 9184.


   Further, printed documents evidencing such posting be attached as one  of
the supporting documents.

   Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal  Accountant  and  HRMO  monitor

observations.recommendation:
1. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require or demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of the financial aid to the latter, pursuant to Section 69 of the GAAM, Volume I.
2. Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor the utilization of the funds released to these Leagues by requiring the recipient Leagues concerned to submit liquidation reports. In this manner, the propriety and validity of the expenditures could be properly evaluated.
3. Require the Leagues concerned to submit a copy of their Constitution and By-laws as basis of reviewing bodies in determining the propriety of the fund release.
4. Further advice all Leagues through its officers to purchase and use the Official Receipt of Republic of the Philippines.
